pipeline.h(3) Library Functions Manual pipeline.h(3)

NAME

pipeline.h - Three-thread render pipeline: capture → display/encode.

SYNOPSIS

Typedefs


typedef struct session_pipeline_s session_pipeline_t

Functions


asciichat_error_t session_pipeline_create (session_capture_ctx_t *capture, session_display_ctx_t *display, session_pipeline_t **out)
asciichat_error_t session_pipeline_run_main (session_pipeline_t *pipeline, session_should_exit_fn should_exit, session_keyboard_handler_fn keyboard_handler, void *user_data)
asciichat_error_t session_pipeline_destroy (session_pipeline_t *pipeline)

Detailed Description

Three-thread render pipeline: capture → display/encode.

Decouples capture, terminal display, and file encoding into separate threads:

  • Capture thread: reads raw image_t frames from media source
  • Main thread: converts to ASCII, prints to terminal (fast)
  • Encode thread: converts to ASCII, encodes to FFmpeg file (slow, non-blocking)

Definition in file pipeline.h.

Typedef Documentation

typedef struct session_pipeline_s session_pipeline_t

Definition at line 22 of file pipeline.h.

Function Documentation

asciichat_error_t session_pipeline_create (session_capture_ctx_t * capture, session_display_ctx_t * display, session_pipeline_t ** out)

Create and start the capture thread and (if --render-file) encode thread.

Parameters

capture Capture context (must have real media_source_t for synchronous mode)
display Display context (may have render_file set for --render-file mode)
out Pointer to receive allocated pipeline context

Returns

ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code otherwise

Note

The pipeline does NOT own capture or display — caller must keep them alive until session_pipeline_destroy() returns.

Definition at line 405 of file pipeline.c.

406                                                                     {
407   if (!capture || !display || !out) {
408     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session_pipeline_create: NULL argument");
409   }
410 
411   session_pipeline_t *p = SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(*p), session_pipeline_t *);
412   p->capture = capture;
413   p->display = display;
414   p->display_queue = frame_queue_create(4, "display");
415   p->encode_queue = frame_queue_create(256, "encode"); // Large buffer for slow encoding
416 
417   // Check if display has render_file configured (will be checked in encode thread)
418   // We store a flag to know whether to enqueue frames for encoding
419   double render_fps = session_display_get_render_fps(display);
420   bool has_file = session_display_has_render_file(display);
421 
422   // Enable encode thread if render_file is available
423   // Note: has_file can be true even if render_file creation failed (ctx->render_file exists)
424   // OR if render_fps is explicitly set for playback speed control
425   p->has_render_file = render_fps > 0 || has_file;
426   log_info("[PIPELINE_CREATE] render_fps=%.1f, has_file=%d, has_render_file=%d", render_fps, has_file ? 1 : 0,
427            p->has_render_file ? 1 : 0);
428 
429   atomic_store_bool(&p->stop, false);
430   atomic_store_u64(&p->first_frame_ns, 0);
431 
432   // Start capture thread
433   if (asciichat_thread_create(&p->capture_tid, "pipeline_capture", pipeline_capture_thread, p) != 0) {
434     frame_queue_destroy(p->display_queue);
435     frame_queue_destroy(p->encode_queue);
436     SAFE_FREE(p);
437     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INIT, "session_pipeline_create: failed to start capture thread");
438   }
439 
440   // Start encode thread only if render_file is active
441   if (p->has_render_file) {
442     if (asciichat_thread_create(&p->encode_tid, "pipeline_encode", pipeline_encode_thread, p) != 0) {
443       atomic_store_bool(&p->stop, true);
444       asciichat_thread_join(&p->capture_tid, NULL);
445       frame_queue_destroy(p->display_queue);
446       frame_queue_destroy(p->encode_queue);
447       SAFE_FREE(p);
448       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INIT, "session_pipeline_create: failed to start encode thread");
449     }
450   }
451 
452   *out = p;
453   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
454 }

    asciichat_thread_join, atomic_store_bool(),
    atomic_store_u64(), session_pipeline_s::capture,
    session_pipeline_s::capture_tid, session_pipeline_s::display,
    session_pipeline_s::display_queue,
    session_pipeline_s::encode_queue,
    session_pipeline_s::encode_tid, ERROR_INIT,
    ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, session_pipeline_s::first_frame_ns,
    session_pipeline_s::has_render_file, log_info,
    SAFE_CALLOC, SAFE_FREE,
    session_display_get_render_fps(),
    session_display_has_render_file(), SET_ERRNO, and
    session_pipeline_s::stop.


Referenced by session_render_loop().






asciichat_error_t session_pipeline_destroy
  (session_pipeline_t * pipeline)


Stop threads and free pipeline.




Waits for capture and encode threads to exit cleanly.




Parameters


pipeline Pipeline context (safe to call with
  NULL)


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK


Definition at line 561 of file pipeline.c.


561                                                                          {
562   if (!pipeline)
563     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
564 
565   log_info("[PIPELINE] Destroying pipeline, waiting for threads...");
566 
567   // Signal threads to stop (may already be stopped)
568   atomic_store_bool(&pipeline->stop, true);
569 
570   // Wake up any threads blocked on condition variable waits
571   // so they can check the stop flag and exit immediately
572   // instead of waiting for timeouts (e.g., 100ms frame_queue_pop timeout)
573   if (pipeline->display_queue) {
574     mutex_lock(&pipeline->display_queue->mu);
575     cond_broadcast(&pipeline->display_queue->not_empty);
576     cond_broadcast(&pipeline->display_queue->not_full);
577     mutex_unlock(&pipeline->display_queue->mu);
578   }
579   if (pipeline->encode_queue) {
580     mutex_lock(&pipeline->encode_queue->mu);
581     cond_broadcast(&pipeline->encode_queue->not_empty);
582     cond_broadcast(&pipeline->encode_queue->not_full);
583     mutex_unlock(&pipeline->encode_queue->mu);
584   }
585 
586   // Drain and wait for capture thread with 1 second timeout
587   if (asciichat_thread_is_initialized(&pipeline->capture_tid)) {
588     int join_result = asciichat_thread_join_timeout(&pipeline->capture_tid, NULL, 1000 * NS_PER_MS_INT);
589     if (join_result != 0) {
590       log_warn("[PIPELINE] Capture thread join timed out or failed (result=%d)", join_result);
591     }
592   }
593 
594   // Drain and wait for encode thread with 1 second timeout
595   if (asciichat_thread_is_initialized(&pipeline->encode_tid)) {
596     int join_result = asciichat_thread_join_timeout(&pipeline->encode_tid, NULL, 1000 * NS_PER_MS_INT);
597     if (join_result != 0) {
598       log_warn("[PIPELINE] Encode thread join timed out or failed (result=%d)", join_result);
599     }
600   }
601 
602   // Flush queues to free any remaining frames
603   frame_queue_flush(pipeline->display_queue, free_frame_generic);
604   frame_queue_flush(pipeline->encode_queue, free_frame_generic);
605 
606   // Don't free queue structures - debug_sync monitoring thread may still be accessing
607   // the condition variables. Queues will be cleaned up with the pipeline structure.
608   SAFE_FREE(pipeline);
609 
610   log_info("[PIPELINE] Pipeline destroyed");
611   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
612 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK,
    asciichat_thread_is_initialized(),
    asciichat_thread_join_timeout(), atomic_store_bool(),
    session_pipeline_s::capture_tid, cond_broadcast(),
    session_pipeline_s::display_queue,
    session_pipeline_s::encode_queue,
    session_pipeline_s::encode_tid, log_info, log_warn,
    frame_queue_s::mu, mutex_lock, mutex_unlock,
    frame_queue_s::not_empty, frame_queue_s::not_full,
    NS_PER_MS_INT, SAFE_FREE, and
  session_pipeline_s::stop.


Referenced by session_render_loop().






asciichat_error_t session_pipeline_run_main
  (session_pipeline_t * pipeline, session_should_exit_fn
  should_exit, session_keyboard_handler_fn keyboard_handler, void *
  user_data)


Main thread loop: drives terminal output and keyboard
  handling.




Pops frames from display queue, converts to ASCII, writes to
    terminal. Handles snapshot delay timer and exit conditions.




Parameters


pipeline Pipeline context created by
  session_pipeline_create()


should_exit Callback to check if rendering should
  stop


keyboard_handler Optional keyboard handler callback


user_data Opaque data passed to callbacks


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success


Note


This function blocks until should_exit() returns
  true or capture ends. Signals capture/encode threads to stop before
  returning.


Definition at line 456 of file pipeline.c.


457                                                                                                            {
458   if (!pipeline || !should_exit) {
459     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session_pipeline_run_main: NULL argument");
460   }
461 
462   log_info("[PIPELINE_MAIN] Starting main thread loop");
463 
464   bool snapshot_mode = GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode);
465   bool snapshot_done = false;
466 
467   while (!should_exit(user_data) && !atomic_load_bool(&pipeline->stop) && !snapshot_done) {
468     bool exit_check = should_exit(user_data);
469     log_debug_every(NS_PER_SEC_INT, "[PIPELINE_DEBUG] should_exit=%d, stop=%d, snapshot_done=%d", exit_check,
470                     atomic_load_bool(&pipeline->stop), snapshot_done);
471 
472     uint64_t pop_time_ns = time_get_ns();
473     pipeline_frame_t *frame = (pipeline_frame_t *)frame_queue_pop(pipeline->display_queue, 1 * NS_PER_MS_INT);
474 
475     if (!frame)
476       continue; // timeout, check should_exit again
477 
478     // Validate frame structure exists before accessing fields
479     if (!frame_is_valid(frame)) {
480       log_error("Received invalid frame: corrupted or NULL");
481       free_frame(frame);
482       frame = NULL;
483       continue;
484     }
485 
486     // Check for EOF sentinel (NULL pixels)
487     if (!frame->pixels) {
488       // EOF sentinel
489       log_info("[PIPELINE_MAIN_EOF] Received EOF sentinel, stopping");
490       free_frame(frame);
491       frame = NULL;
492       break;
493     }
494 
495     // Log frame pop with timestamp
496     log_info("[PIPELINE_MAIN_POP] frame=%p, pixels=%p, w=%d, h=%d at %llu ns", (void *)frame, (void *)frame->pixels,
497              frame->w, frame->h, (unsigned long long)pop_time_ns);
498 
499     // Check if help screen is active - if so, don't render ASCII frames
500     bool help_is_active = pipeline->display && keyboard_help_is_active(pipeline->display);
501 
502     if (!help_is_active) {
503       log_info("[PIPELINE_MAIN_RENDER] Converting frame to ASCII: %dx%d", frame->w, frame->h);
504       // Convert to ASCII
505       image_t tmp = {.w = frame->w, .h = frame->h, .pixels = (rgb_pixel_t *)frame->pixels};
506       char *ascii = session_display_convert_to_ascii(pipeline->display, &tmp);
507       free_frame(frame);
508       frame = NULL;
509 
510       if (ascii) {
511         // Write ASCII to terminal
512         session_display_write_ascii(pipeline->display, ascii);
513         SAFE_FREE(ascii);
514       }
515     } else {
516       free_frame(frame);
517       frame = NULL;
518     }
519 
520     // Keyboard polling
521     if (keyboard_handler) {
522       keyboard_key_t key = keyboard_read_nonblocking();
523       if (key != KEY_NONE) {
524         log_debug("PIPELINE_KEYBOARD: Received key=%d", key);
525         keyboard_handler(NULL, (int)key, user_data); // NULL capture ctx
526       }
527     }
528 
529     // Snapshot mode: check if elapsed time has reached snapshot_delay duration
530     // This check runs every iteration after first frame is rendered (when display.c sets
531     // g_snapshot_first_frame_rendered)
532     if (snapshot_mode && !snapshot_done) {
533       if (g_snapshot_first_frame_rendered && g_snapshot_first_frame_rendered_ns > 0) {
534         double snapshot_delay = GET_OPTION(snapshot_delay);
535         uint64_t now_ns = time_get_ns();
536         uint64_t elapsed_ns = now_ns - g_snapshot_first_frame_rendered_ns;
537         double elapsed_sec = (double)elapsed_ns / (double)NS_PER_SEC_INT;
538 
539         if ((unsigned long)(elapsed_sec * 10) % 10 == 0 || elapsed_sec < 0.2) {
540           log_info("[SNAPSHOT_PIPELINE] CHECK: elapsed=%.3f target=%.2f", elapsed_sec, snapshot_delay);
541         }
542 
543         // snapshot_delay=0 means exit after first frame
544         // snapshot_delay>0 means wait that many seconds before exiting
545         bool should_snapshot_exit = (snapshot_delay == 0.0) || (elapsed_sec >= snapshot_delay);
546 
547         if (should_snapshot_exit) {
548           log_info("[SNAPSHOT_PIPELINE] EXITING - elapsed=%.3f target=%.2f", elapsed_sec, snapshot_delay);
549           snapshot_done = true;
550         }
551       }
552     }
553   }
554 
555   log_info("[PIPELINE_MAIN] Main loop exiting, signaling threads to stop");
556   atomic_store_bool(&pipeline->stop, true);
557 
558   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
559 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK, atomic_load_bool(),
    atomic_store_bool(), session_pipeline_s::display,
    session_pipeline_s::display_queue, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    frame, g_snapshot_first_frame_rendered,
    g_snapshot_first_frame_rendered_ns, GET_OPTION,
    keyboard_help_is_active(), keyboard_read_nonblocking(),
    log_debug, log_debug_every, log_error, log_info,
    NS_PER_MS_INT, NS_PER_SEC_INT, SAFE_FREE,
    session_display_convert_to_ascii(),
    session_display_write_ascii(), SET_ERRNO,
    should_exit(), session_pipeline_s::stop, and
    time_get_ns().
